entries.description: The Rust SDK host surface accepts or wraps a pre-built core Runtime while other SDKs construct the native runtime through FFI bindings.
entries.parity_scope: Runtime decisions, effects, resource limits, and reserved error reasons remain canonical.
entries.allowed_difference: Perf telemetry and dispatchers may be selected before SDK wrapper construction rather than through an FFI constructor parameter.

entries.description: The Node facade exposes JavaScript camelCase result properties and enum members while native JSON remains snake_case.
entries.parity_scope: Wire values for decisions, intervention points, enforcement modes, error reasons, and effects remain canonical.
entries.allowed_difference: Facade property names such as transformedPolicyTarget and policyInput differ from native transformed_policy_target and policy_input.

entries.description: The Python facade uses Pythonic snake_case dataclass properties and enum members over the native runtime JSON.
entries.parity_scope: Wire values for decisions, intervention points, enforcement modes, error reasons, and effects remain canonical.
entries.allowed_difference: Enum member names are uppercase while enum values are canonical lower case strings.

entries.description: The .NET facade uses PascalCase types and properties while preserving canonical JSON values at the runtime boundary.
entries.parity_scope: Wire values for decisions, intervention points, enforcement modes, error reasons, and effects remain canonical.
entries.allowed_difference: Public CLR member names follow .NET naming conventions.

entries.description: Core owns event construction but hosts and integrations own exporter plumbing and provider naming.
entries.parity_scope: Redaction, low cardinality event content, and perf telemetry levels remain canonical.
entries.allowed_difference: Exporter resource attributes and provider labels may differ by host integration.

entries.description: Python and Node parity tests require their native extension build products while Rust core tests run directly.
entries.parity_scope: When the native extension is present, evaluated decisions and reasons must match the shared fixtures.
entries.allowed_difference: Language test harnesses may skip native runtime checks when the extension is not built by that harness.
